response:
Let’s check if \( 20369 \) is prime.  

First, test small primes:  
- Divisible by 2? No (odd).  
- Divisible by 3? Sum of digits \( 2+0+3+6+9 = 20 \), not divisible by 3.  
- Divisible by 5? Last digit not 0 or 5.  
- Divisible by 7? \( 20369 \div 7 \approx 2909.857... \) not integer.  
- Divisible by 11? Alternating sum \( 2 - 0 + 3 - 6 + 9 = 8 \), not divisible by 11.  
- Divisible by 13? \( 13 \times 1566 = 20358 \), remainder 11, so no.  
- Divisible by 17? \( 17 \times 1198 = 20366 \), remainder 3, so no.  
- Divisible by 19? \( 19 \times 1072 = 20368 \), remainder 1, so no.  
- Divisible by 23? \( 23 \times 885 = 20355 \), remainder 14, so no.  
- Divisible by 29? \( 29 \times 702 = 20358 \), remainder 11, so no.  
- Divisible by 31? \( 31 \times 657 = 20367 \), remainder 2, so no.  
- Divisible by 37? \( 37 \times 550 = 20350 \), remainder 19, so no.  
- Divisible by 41? \( 41 \times 497 = 20377 \) (too high), \( 41 \times 496 = 20336 \), remainder 33, so no.  
- Divisible by 43? \( 43 \times 473 = 20339 \), remainder 30, so no.  
- Divisible by 47? \( 47 \times 433 = 20351 \), remainder 18, so no.  

We need to check up to \( \sqrt{20369} \approx 142.7 \).  

Check 53: \( 53 \times 384 = 20352 \), remainder 17, no.  
Check 59: \( 59 \times 345 = 20355 \), remainder 14, no.  
Check 61: \( 61 \times 334 = 20374 \) (too high), \( 61 \times 333 = 20313 \), remainder 56, no.  
Check 67: \( 67 \times 304 = 20368 \), remainder 1, no.  
Check 71: \( 71 \times 287 = 20377 \) (too high), \( 71 \times 286 = 20306 \), remainder 63, no.  
Check 73: \( 73 \times 279 = 20367 \), remainder 2, no.  
Check 79: \( 79 \times 258 = 20382 \) (too high), \( 79 \times 257 = 20303 \), remainder 66, no.  
Check 83: \( 83 \times 245 = 20335 \), remainder 34, no.  
Check 89: \( 89 \times 229 = 20381 \) (too high), \( 89 \times 228 = 20292 \), remainder 77, no.  
Check 97: \( 97 \times 210 = 20370 \) (too high), \( 97 \times 209 = 20273 \), remainder 96, no.  
Check 101: \( 101 \times 201 = 20301 \), remainder 68, no.  
Check 103: \( 103 \times 197 = 20291 \), remainder 78, no.  
Check 107: \( 107 \times 190 = 20330 \), remainder 39, no.  
Check 109: \( 109 \times 187 = 20383 \) (too high), \( 109 \times 186 = 20274 \), remainder 95, no.  
Check 113: \( 113 \times 180 = 20340 \), remainder 29, no.  
Check 127: \( 127 \times 160 = 20320 \), remainder 49, no.  
Check 131: \( 131 \times 155 = 20305 \), remainder 64, no.  
Check 137: \( 137 \times 148 = 20276 \), remainder 93, no.  
Check 139: \( 139 \times 146 = 20294 \), remainder 75, no.  

We’ve checked all primes up to 139, and none divide 20369.  
Since \( \sqrt{20369} \approx 142.7 \), the next prime to check is 149, but \( 149 > 142.7 \), so we can stop.  

Thus, **20369 is prime**.  

**Answer:** yes
